meta.name: GAD-7 Generalized Anxiety Disorder Scale
meta.description: The GAD-7 is a concise and efficient self-report screening tool for anxiety symptoms, consisting of 7 items used to assess the severity of generalized anxiety symptoms over the past two weeks. It can also be used to screen for panic disorder, social anxiety, and PTSD.
meta.author: Spitzer, Kroenke, Williams & Löwe (2006)
meta.reference: Spitzer RL, Kroenke K, Williams JBW, Löwe B. A brief measure for assessing generalized anxiety disorder: the GAD-7. Arch Intern Med. 2006;166(10):1092-1097.
meta.instruction: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems?

questions.text: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying
Worrying too much about different things
Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your anxiety score is within the normal range. No significant anxiety symptoms detected.
suggestions: Practice relaxation activities such as deep breathing or meditation
Keep up a regular exercise routine

ranges.label: Mild Anxiety
ranges.description: You may be experiencing some mild anxiety symptoms that may slightly affect daily life.
suggestions: Learn and practice relaxation techniques (e.g., progressive muscle relaxation, mindfulness meditation)
Try recording situations that trigger anxiety and analyze them rationally
If symptoms persist, consider consulting a professional

ranges.label: Moderate Anxiety
ranges.description: Your anxiety symptoms are at a moderate level and may noticeably affect work, social life, and daily activities.
suggestions: Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for anxiety
Maintain regular sleep and exercise routines
Avoid excessive use of alcohol or sleep medications

ranges.label: Severe Anxiety
ranges.description: Your anxiety symptoms are quite severe and may significantly affect daily functioning. Please seek professional help promptly.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek medical attention from a psychiatrist
Medication combined with psychotherapy may be needed
Ensure you have a reliable support system
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face anxiety-provoking situations under professional guidance
